palette: Oxidized copper, parchment, deep navy, and Victorian ochre. The colors of a 17th-century library under a late-afternoon March sun—warm, dusty, but with a sharp metallic edge from the sensor array.
lighting: Low-angle, atmospheric street-level light. Long shadows across cobblestones, flickering fluorescent light in the subterranean 'Netty' suite, and the soft, diffuse glow of old stone under a grey Oxford sky.
instagram_prompts: First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture from ground level. A close-up view of weathered Oxford cobblestones on St Giles' street, looking up toward the massive, blurry pillars of the Ashmolean Museum. The world feels enormous. Oxidized copper and deep navy tones. No people, just the overwhelming scale of the architecture. World is impossibly large.

First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture from a low angle inside a subterranean room. The texture of original Victorian floor tiles—geometric, cream and ochre—is hyper-detailed. A single narrow slit of a street-level window shows the dome of the Radcliffe Camera in the far distance, framed by the legs of a wooden chair. Dusty, atmospheric lighting. World is impossibly large.

First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture at night. A close-up of a rusted iron gate leading to an Oxford college quad. Through the bars, the soft, warm glow of a library window illuminates falling mist. Parchment and deep navy palette. The camera is positioned low, looking through the intricate metalwork. World is impossibly large.
